tools.content: When an 82-year-old Kentucky woman was offered $26 million from an AI company that wanted to build a data center on her land, she said no. Sure, that same company can try to rezone 2,000 acres nearby anyway, but as AI infrastructure stretches further into the real world, the real world is starting to push back.  That tension is everywhere […]

tools.title: The latest in data centers, AI, and energy
tools.summary: Massive new data centers are the physical foundation for tech companies’ hopes and dreams for AI. But the rush to expand warehouses full of energy-hungry servers has also kicked up fights across the world over their impact on power grids, utility bil
tools.content:
Massive new data centers are the physical foundation for tech companies’ hopes and dreams for AI. But the rush to expand warehouses full of energy-hungry servers has also kicked up fights across the world over their impact on power grids, utility bills, nearby communities, and the environment. 
From audacious plans to launch data centers into space to the latest legal battles over pollution, The Verge has the biggest news and reporting surrounding data centers.

creative.title: Meta's hyperagents improve at tasks and improve at improving
creative.summary: Researchers at Meta and several universities have developed "hyperagents," AI systems that don't just solve tasks, but also optimize the very mechanism they use to get better. The approach works across different task areas and could open the door to
creative.content:
Researchers at Meta and several universities have developed "hyperagents," AI systems that don't just solve tasks, but also optimize the very mechanism they use to get better. The approach works across different task areas and could open the door to self-accelerating AI.
The article Meta's hyperagents improve at tasks and improve at improving appeared first on The Decoder.

creative.title: OpenAI sets two-stage Sora shutdown with app closing April 2026 and API following in September
creative.summary:
OpenAI is pulling the plug on Sora, its AI video generation tool, in a two-stage shutdown starting April 2026. The move signals a major strategic shift away from creative AI tools and toward coding and enterprise products.

culture.title: ‘They feel true’: political deepfakes are growing in influence – even if people know they aren’t real
culture.summary:
AI images of people – such as women in military contexts – are making money and serving as propaganda, researchers say
Online content creators are not just building fake images and videos of prominent public figures, they are also fabricating people
culture.content:
AI images of people – such as women in military contexts – are making money and serving as propaganda, researchers say
Online content creators are not just building fake images and videos of prominent public figures, they are also fabricating people and using them in military contexts, which can make them money and even serve as effective propaganda, according to artificial intelligence researchers.
Some of these online avatars are sexualized images of women wearing camouflage garb that have gen

culture.content:
Do we really need a McDonald’s CEO fronting ads or a Gianni Infantino Panini sticker? No. But in the age of Trump, the boss class feels emboldened
A few weeks ago, the CEO of McDonald’s appeared in a video sampling the chain’s new “Big Arch burger”. culture.summary:
The US court verdicts declaring Meta liable for getting people addicted and ruining lives must be just the start of a global fightback
Good news is so rare these days, you don’t quite know how to take it. You want to celebrate, but a rival instinct t
culture.content:
The US court verdicts declaring Meta liable for getting people addicted and ruining lives must be just the start of a global fightback
Good news is so rare these days, you don’t quite know how to take it. You want to celebrate, but a rival instinct tells you it’ll be pulled back somehow, the same feeling you get when your team scores a late winner, but you’re filled with instant dread that the goal will be overturned on a video replay.
I confess that is how I responded to the double legal blow d

culture.title: Wikipedia bans AI-generated content in its online encyclopedia
culture.summary:
Ban includes two exceptions: AI can still be used for translations, and to make minor copy edits
Wikipedia has banned the use of artificial intelligence in the generation or rewriting of content for its voluminous online encyclopedia.
In a recent pol
culture.content:
Ban includes two exceptions: AI can still be used for translations, and to make minor copy edits
Wikipedia has banned the use of artificial intelligence in the generation or rewriting of content for its voluminous online encyclopedia.
In a recent policy change, Wikipedia said that the use of large language models (or LLMs) “often violates” its core principles and will not be allowed. The English language version of Wikipedia has more than 7.1m articles.
 Continue reading...

culture.title: Number of AI chatbots ignoring human instructions increasing, study says
culture.summary:
Exclusive: Research finds sharp rise in models evading safeguards and destroying emails without permission
AI models that lie and cheat appear to be growing in number with reports of deceptive scheming surging in the last six months, a study into the
culture.content:
Exclusive: Research finds sharp rise in models evading safeguards and destroying emails without permission
AI models that lie and cheat appear to be growing in number with reports of deceptive scheming surging in the last six months, a study into the technology has found.
AI chatbots and agents disregarded direct instructions, evaded safeguards and deceived humans and other AI, according to research funded by the UK government-funded AI Security Institute (AISI). The study, shared with the Guard
